ynjtK79qJF.title: The Pipeline
slides:
# The Pipeline

3 TRT inference models process every cardiac ultrasound frame:

| Model | Task | Relative Size |
|-------|------|---------------|
| **plax_inference** | Chamber segmentation | Large |
| **aortic_inference** | Stenosis classification | Small |
| **bmode_inference** | Perspective classification | Large |

Under **Event scheduler** — all 3 run on GPU **simultaneously**.
Under **Greedy scheduler** — they run **one after another**.

Single-slot queues: the frame moves as fast as the **slowest model**.

# What is Green Context?

**Without GC (nGC):** 3 models share all SMs. Free-for-all — one model can hog SMs, starving others.

**With GC (pGC):** Each model gets a **guaranteed SM partition**. No interference.

| Mode | PLAX | Aortic | B-Mode | Default |
|------|------|--------|--------|---------|
| nGC | shared | shared | shared | shared |
| pGC | 4 SMs | 4 SMs | 6 SMs | 2 SMs |

Trade-off: fewer SMs per model (slower) but **no interference** (predictable).

# Why SM Count Matters **SM** = Streaming Multiprocessor. More SMs = faster kernels. | GPU | SMs | pGC per model | pGC overhead | |-----|-----|---------------|-------------| | RTX 6000 Ada | **142** | 44-52 SMs | +100% GPU time | | Jetson Orin | **16** | 4-6 SMs | ~0% | At 142 SMs: models have plenty. Partitioning wastes compute. At 16 SMs: models already limited. Partitioning is **free** — isolation without cost. **Hypothesis:** SM scarcity makes isolation matter.

# Experiment Setup

Can't run on Orin yet (needs OS reflash). Used **CUDA MPS** to limit RTX 6000 to 16 SMs.

| Experiment | SMs | Method | Frames |
|-----------|-----|--------|--------|
| v8_20k_clocked | 142 | Full GPU | ~1600-20000 |
| v20_mps_small_gpu | **16** | MPS 12% | ~1900 |

4 configs each: Event nGC, Event pGC, Greedy nGC, Greedy pGC.

Event scheduler is where models **compete** for SMs.

# Key Metrics: Spread

**Spread** = max − min GPU time across 3 models in one frame.

| Frame | Aortic | PLAX | B-Mode | Spread |
|-------|--------|------|--------|--------|
| #100 | 5ms | **9ms** | 4ms | **5ms** (unfair!) |
| #101 | 5ms | 6ms | 6ms | **1ms** (fair) |

Low spread = models finish together = smooth pipeline flow.

# Key Metrics: Correlation

**Pearson r** — do two models' GPU times move together?

| r value | Meaning |
|---------|---------|
| **+0.9** | PLAX slow → BMode slow (move together) |
| **0.0** | Independent |
| **-0.5** | PLAX fast → BMode slow (SM stealing!) |

**Spearman ρ** — same idea but uses *ranks* instead of raw values. More robust to bimodal distributions. When they disagree, trust Spearman.

**P99/P50 ratio** — how bad is the worst 1% vs typical? 1.0x = perfect. 2.0x = bad tail.

# Frame Fairness Scatter

![](https://pub-6528bbdebaed4b9997412b6948ed7524.r2.dev/presentations/ynjtK79qJF/1_fairness_scatter.png)

Each dot = one frame. X = PLAX, Y = BMode GPU time.

**142 SM:** Low correlation (~0). Plenty of SMs — no competition.

**16 SM:** High positive correlation (r~0.9) in both nGC and pGC. Similar-sized models respond to the same constraint. But **Aortic** decouples: Spearman ρ goes from -0.35 (nGC) to -0.27 (pGC).

# Per-Frame Spread

![](https://pub-6528bbdebaed4b9997412b6948ed7524.r2.dev/presentations/ynjtK79qJF/2_spread_timeseries.png)

Lower + flatter = smoother frame flow.

| Experiment | nGC P50 | pGC P50 | Reduction |
|-----------|---------|---------|-----------|
| 142 SM | 1.84ms | 1.38ms | **-25%** |
| 16 SM | 2.01ms | 1.75ms | **-13%** |

pGC makes frames more even at both GPU sizes.

# GPU Distributions Overlaid

![](https://pub-6528bbdebaed4b9997412b6948ed7524.r2.dev/presentations/ynjtK79qJF/3_overlaid_distributions.png)

Blue fill = nGC. Orange line = pGC. Same axes.

**142 SM:** pGC shifts right (slower — fewer SMs) but tighter.

**16 SM:** nGC and pGC overlap (same speed). But pGC P99 lines are **left** of nGC → shorter tails. Isolation is free AND trims worst-case.

# Frame Progress Heatmap

![](https://pub-6528bbdebaed4b9997412b6948ed7524.r2.dev/presentations/ynjtK79qJF/4_frame_heatmap.png)

Each column = one frame. Each row = one model. Color = GPU time. Shared color scale per row.

**Streaks** = shared slowdown. **Spots** = unfairness (one model dark, others light).

# Tail Bound: P99/P50 Ratio ![](https://pub-6528bbdebaed4b9997412b6948ed7524.r2.dev/presentations/ynjtK79qJF/5_tail_bounds.png) Lower = more predictable. **The crossover:** | Model | 142 SM nGC→pGC | 16 SM nGC→pGC | |-------|----------------|---------------| | Aortic | 1.20x → 1.29x (worse) | 1.47x → 1.34x (-9%) | | PLAX | 1.61x → 1.79x (worse) | 1.34x → 1.20x (-10%) | | B-Mode | 1.17x → 1.19x (flat) | 1.34x → 1.14x (-15%) | At 142 SMs, pGC hurts. At 16 SMs, pGC **helps all models**.

# The Crossover ![](https://pub-6528bbdebaed4b9997412b6948ed7524.r2.dev/presentations/ynjtK79qJF/6_crossover_summary.png) **Top:** Overhead drops from +20-117% → ~0% at 16 SMs. **Middle:** P99 improvement flips from negative to +8-17%. Lines cross zero. **Bottom:** Spread reduction is positive at both sizes (pGC always fairer).

# Key Numbers | Metric | 142 SM nGC | 142 SM pGC | 16 SM nGC | 16 SM pGC | |--------|-----------|-----------|----------|----------| | GPU overhead | — | +20-117% | — | ~0% | | Spread P50 | 1.84ms | 1.38ms | 2.01ms | 1.75ms | | PLAX P99/P50 | 1.61x | 1.79x | 1.34x | 1.20x | | BMode P99/P50 | 1.17x | 1.19x | 1.34x | 1.14x | | Aortic P99/P50 | 1.20x | 1.29x | 1.47x | 1.34x | | PLAX-BMode ρ | 0.24 | 0.73 | 0.85 | 0.86 | | Aort-BMode ρ | 0.38 | 0.72 | -0.35 | -0.27 |

# What We Learned 1. **pGC is free at 16 SMs** — zero overhead. No reason not to use it. 2. **Tail latency improves 9-15%** — P99/P50 ratio drops for all models. 3. **Per-frame spread reduces 13%** — models finish more evenly. 4. **SM stealing is visible** — Aortic-BMode ρ goes negative at 16 SMs under nGC. pGC reduces it. 5. **PLAX and BMode stay coupled** — similar models respond to same load. GC can't break this. 6. **BGL limits Event E2E** — CUDA driver mutex makes Event 2x slower than Greedy.

# Caveats & Next Steps **Caveats:** - MPS ≠ native small GPU (cache/bandwidth still 142-SM class) - 1900 frames — statistically thin for P99 claims - Single run, no randomization, no confidence intervals **Next steps:** 1. Flash Orin → native 16 SM experiment 2. Sweep SM budgets (16, 24, 32, 48, 72 SMs) 3. Longer runs (20K frames) with replicates 4. Bootstrap CIs on all metrics
